source_of_truth: Birchal-issued copy on join.birchal.com (sales-skill repo, web/campaigns/BIRCH/meta.json) + BIRCH/quotes.md (verbatim, 6 May 2026 from Kirstin) + BIRCH/LESSONS.md (5-7 May 2026 revision pass)

facts.claim: 324 Australian companies have raised on Birchal since 2018
facts.source: join.birchal.com hero subhead + welcome email body (verified 2026-05-07)
facts.note: User-confirmed 2026-05-07. Use '324 Australian companies' verbatim. Acceptable variants: '324 companies', '324 raises', '324 Aussie brands'. Always pair with 'on Birchal' or 'through Birchal' to avoid implying these are unrelated raises.

facts.claim: $234M+ raised on Birchal across all CSF campaigns to date
facts.source: join.birchal.com hero subhead + TRUST_STAT_1_VALUE in BIRCH/meta.json (verified 2026-05-07)
facts.note: Always include the '+' suffix to indicate cumulative-and-growing. Acceptable: '$234M+ raised', 'more than $234M', 'over $234M'. Never round up to $235M or down to $230M.

facts.claim: 137,000 individual investments made on Birchal since 2018
facts.source: join.birchal.com hero subhead + TRUST_STAT_3_VALUE in BIRCH/meta.json (verified 2026-05-07)
facts.note: Birchal-issued copy uses both '137,000 investors' (hero short) and '137,000 individual investments' (welcome email). Both acceptable. Investments != unique investors strictly, but Birchal uses 'investors' as shorthand. Do not invent unique-investor counts.

facts.claim: Birchal has been operating CSF since 2018, eight years of platform traction at time of writing (2026)
facts.note: 'Eight years' is the user-facing framing. Spell out 'eight' in body copy; '8 years' acceptable in headline-only contexts.

facts.claim: Naked Life raised $2.95M on Birchal in July 2023
facts.note: Verified-good raise. Safe to use as social proof in ad copy.

facts.claim: Our Cow raised $2M on Birchal in March 2023
facts.note: Verified-good raise.

facts.claim: Medigrowth raised $1.59M on Birchal in March 2025
facts.note: Verified-good raise. Australian medicinal cannabis grower.

facts.claim: Earthletica raised $1.1M on Birchal in April 2026
facts.note: Most recent verified raise. Pair with Chris Raleigh testimonial (QUOTE-005) for strong recency proof.

facts.claim: Aquafab raised $600K on Birchal in March 2026
Pulse Tile raised $400K on Birchal in March 2026
Reckless Brewery raised $513K on Birchal in March 2026
Companies in administration must NOT be used as social proof
facts.source: BIRCH/LESSONS.md item 5 of CSF compliance section (2026-05-07)
facts.note: BLOCK list. Zero Co (raised $5M from 3,107 investors) and Old Young's are both in administration. Do not feature in ads even though their raises were successful at the time. Always check live company page status before featuring any new brand.

facts.claim: CSF short-form risk warning, byte-exact
facts.verbatim_text: *Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.
facts.source: FMTH live ad (clients/farm-thru/loop/live-ad-test.json) + BIRCH/LESSONS.md item 2 (footer FOOTER_DISCLAIMER is canonical for landing pages, this short-form is canonical for ads)
facts.note: Pasted verbatim into every Meta primary_text as the final line. Leading asterisk + sentence-case + full stop, no Oxford comma, no 'Birchal' substitution. Must be byte-exact match — compliance script blocks ads where this line is missing or paraphrased.

facts.claim: Use 'gain access to' or 'view the offer document', never 'we will send you the offer document'
facts.contradicts_if: (we will send you (the|our) offer document|we'll send you (the|our) offer)
facts.note: Birchal does not email the offer document. It goes live on the offer page when the round opens. Acceptable verbs: 'gain access to', 'view', 'read', 'follow'. Banned phrases: 'we will send you the offer', 'we'll email you the offer'. Acceptable to say 'we will tell you the moment the offer goes live' (informing about the launch is fine, just not sending the document).

facts.claim: ASIC RG 234 + INFO 217 + s 738G banned phrases for pre-offer CSF marketing
facts.contradicts_if: (\bbe first to invest\b|\bfirst in line\b|\binvest from \$|\bfrom as little as \$|\bguaranteed return\b|\bsecure your allocation\b|\bskip the queue\b|\bjump the queue\b|\binvest now\b|\bbuy now\b|\bsubscribe now\b)
facts.source: web/scripts/validate_client.py CSF banned-phrase blocklist + ASIC RG 234 + INFO 217 + Corps Act s 738G (anti-preferential-terms)
facts.note: HARD BLOCK. Any of these phrases in primary_text, headline, or description = ad fails compliance. Approved alternatives: 'Save my spot', 'Follow the campaign', 'Read the offer when it opens', 'Be on the waitlist', 'Hear when the offer goes live'. Do not imply preferential terms vs the public offer (s 738G anti-preferential-terms rule).

facts.claim: Pre-offer ads use waitlist verbs only (follow, save, read, hear), never transaction verbs (invest, buy, subscribe)
facts.source: ASIC INFO 217 (pre-offer CSF marketing) + DRC SKILL.md regulated-industry handoff
facts.note: Approved CTAs for waitlist phase: 'Save my spot' (primary), 'Follow campaign', 'Read the offer when it opens', 'Hear when the offer goes live'. Once the offer document is live on Birchal's site, the rules change but we are not at that stage.

facts.claim: Dom Pym (Co-founder Up, Triple Bubble) — primary investor-validation quote
facts.verbatim_text: I've looked at Birchal from the founder's seat and the investor's seat, and from both angles the answer is the same. Birchal is one of the most important companies in Australian fintech.
facts.note: First two sentences of the full Pym quote. The 'founder's seat / investor's seat' line is the strongest single line in the deck. Acceptable to use the first sentence alone. Do not paraphrase. Attribute as 'Dom Pym, Co-founder Up + Triple Bubble' or 'Dom Pym, Triple Bubble'.

facts.claim: Nick Carter (Triple Bubble) — fund-manager validation quote
facts.verbatim_text: Equity crowdfunding is core fintech infrastructure, the rails that let everyday Australians invest in the businesses they believe in, and Birchal has built the dominant platform in the category.
facts.note: Mid-quote excerpt, lightly punctuation-edited (em-dashes converted to commas). Original uses em-dashes; ad copy must not. Attribute as 'Nick Carter, Triple Bubble'.

facts.claim: Don McKenzie (Tribe Global) — VC-CSF bridge quote
facts.verbatim_text: Crowdfunding and venture capital shouldn't be rival pathways, they should reinforce each other. Birchal is leading the way in improving how CSF operates in Australia.
facts.note: First and last sentences combined, em-dashes converted to commas. Attribute as 'Don McKenzie, Tribe Global'.

facts.claim: Shayne Gary (Australia Venture Partners Fund) — operator validation
facts.verbatim_text: We first backed Kirstin as investors through the Techstars accelerator, and everything since has confirmed why we did. She's a category-defining operator with an unusually clear view of where the Australian capital markets need to go.
facts.note: Verbatim. Attribute as 'Shayne Gary, Australia Venture Partners Fund'.

facts.claim: Chris Raleigh (Earthletica founder, $1.1M April 2026) — recent founder testimonial
facts.verbatim_text: We loved working with the Birchal team. The process was seamless and the support throughout the campaign was exceptional. Kirstin is one of the best capital raising advisors in Australia right now.
facts.note: Verbatim. Note 'seamless' is normally a banned AI-tell word but is verbatim from the testimonial source — acceptable inside attributed quotation marks only. Attribute as 'Chris Raleigh, Earthletica'. Pairs with RAISE-004 (Earthletica $1.1M, April 2026) for recency proof.

facts.claim: Birchal is running its own CSF campaign on its platform; offer goes live 26 June 2026
facts.note: The offer document goes live on the Birchal offer page on 26 June 2026. Waitlist signups are gathered before that date. Acceptable framings: 'Birchal is opening its own raise', 'Birchal is running a CSF campaign on the platform it built'. Do not give specific raise amount or valuation — those are in the offer document, not yet public.

facts.claim: Approved waitlist CTAs and microcopy
facts.note: Primary CTA across the BIRCH landing page is 'Save my spot' (header, sticky, signup card). Use this verbatim in ad CTAs. Acceptable variants in body copy: 'Save your spot on the waitlist', 'Be on the waitlist', 'Hear when the offer goes live'.

facts.claim: Aubrey Blanche, on why crowdfunding matters (CSF category validation, NOT a Birchal claim)
facts.verbatim_text: There are a ton of reasons that crowdfunding is not only a good–but sometimes the best–option for businesses. It can democratise ownership, provide capital without requiring unrealistic growth targets, and is more available to non-traditional founders. In short, it's an excellent vehicle to bring more businesses online, which can only be seen as a good thing. Crowdfunding–whether through equity, reward, donation, or debt–opens up the ability to invest in excellent businesses to a significantly broader set of investors.
facts.ad_safe_text: Crowdfunding opens up the ability to invest in excellent businesses to a significantly broader set of investors.
facts.source: https://www.startupdaily.net/advice/opinion/why-crowdfunding-is-an-important-part-of-australias-capital-mix/ (client-supplied 2026-05-17, Kirstin Hunter; published opinion piece by Aubrey Blanche)
facts.note: Third-party validation of the CSF category, NOT a Birchal-specific claim. Attribute exactly as 'Aubrey Blanche' (no invented title). Use the ad_safe_text excerpt (contiguous sub-span) in ads; verbatim_text uses en-dashes and is the integrity record only. Does NOT license any market-share, valuation, '#1', 'largest', 'leading', or 'first' numeric claim. Opinion/vision only.

facts.claim: Kirstin Hunter (CEO, Birchal), Forbes: mission beyond numbers / vision
facts.verbatim_text: Birchal's mission goes beyond numbers: it's about fostering an innovation ecosystem where every investor, entrepreneur, and idea can find a place to thrive. Crowdsourced funding represents a paradigm shift towards inclusivity and breadth. By empowering a diverse community of investors, Birchal enables founders from all backgrounds – women, people of colour, LGBTQIA+ individuals, migrants, and First Nations people – to draw on the power of the crowd to access capital and realise their entrepreneurial visions. Successful crowdsourced funding campaigns result in hundreds, if not thousands, of individual investors putting their money behind ideas they believe in. And when those investments pay off, we see hundreds, if not thousands, of winners.
facts.ad_safe_text: Birchal's mission goes beyond numbers: it's about fostering an innovation ecosystem where every investor, entrepreneur, and idea can find a place to thrive.
facts.source: https://www.forbes.com.au/news/leadership/crowdsourcing-our-way-to-economic-growth-and-diversity-in-startups/ (client-supplied 2026-05-17, Kirstin Hunter wrote 'Forbes quotes from me'; published)
facts.note: Vision / cause-purpose angle. Pairs with the BIRCHAL-NEXT positioning. Attribute exactly 'Kirstin Hunter, CEO, Birchal'. Use ad_safe_text (contiguous first sentence) in ads; verbatim_text uses en-dashes and contains banned AI-tell words ('fostering', 'ecosystem', 'thrive', 'paradigm', 'empowering') which are exempt ONLY inside this attributed verbatim quotation, never in surrounding prose. Opinion/vision only; no numeric claim licensed.

facts.claim: Kirstin Hunter (CEO, Birchal), on retail investor power + CEO-appointment bio (co-founder Future Super, ex-Techstars)
facts.verbatim_text: What really excites me is crowdsourced equity increases the economic power of retail investors by taking business funding decisions away from a small number of professional investors and placing it in the hands of the community. As the new CEO of Birchal, I'm thrilled to leverage my experience as co-founder of Future Super combining people and financial power to change the superannuation sector, and more recently shaping the next generation of innovative business leaders as an investor at Techstars Tech Central Sydney Accelerator.
facts.ad_safe_text: Crowdsourced equity increases the economic power of retail investors by taking business funding decisions away from a small number of professional investors and placing it in the hands of the community.
facts.source: https://www.startupdaily.net/topic/people/former-techstars-boss-kirstin-hunter-takes-on-birchal-ceo-role/ (client-supplied 2026-05-17, Kirstin Hunter wrote 'Me taking on Birchal'; published)
facts.note: Democratisation / retail-investor-power angle (Segment A) + Kirstin bio (co-founder Future Super, investor at Techstars Tech Central Sydney Accelerator). Attribute exactly 'Kirstin Hunter, CEO, Birchal'. The published source's trailing ', she said.' is the publication's attribution tag, NOT part of the quote. Published bio sentence contains a double space in 'my  experience'; ad_safe collapses to single space. ad_safe_text is the retail-investor-power excerpt (contiguous). 'leverage' / 'innovative' in the bio sentence are banned AI-tells, exempt ONLY inside this attributed verbatim, never in surrounding prose. Opinion/bio only; no numeric claim licensed.

facts.claim: Kirstin Hunter (CEO, Birchal), on CSF + the Denholm R&D report (category/policy vision)
facts.verbatim_text: Crowdsourced funding sits at the intersection of two of the report's central ambitions: mobilising more private capital for innovation, and democratising access to that capital. When retail investors – not just wholesale investors and VCs – can back the next generation of Australian deeptech, medtech, and cleantech companies we get a richer, more resilient innovation ecosystem.
facts.ad_safe_text: When retail investors, not just wholesale investors and VCs, can back the next generation of Australian deeptech, medtech, and cleantech companies we get a richer, more resilient innovation ecosystem.
facts.note: Vision / category-building / policy angle (Segment B, sophisticated). Attribute exactly 'Kirstin Hunter, CEO, Birchal'. Use ad_safe_text (en-dashes converted to commas, contiguous span) in ads; verbatim_text is the integrity record. 'ecosystem' is a banned AI-tell, exempt ONLY inside this attributed verbatim, never in surrounding prose. Policy framing, no numeric claim licensed.

facts.claim: Backing female founders (supporting reference link for the diversity/inclusion angle; NOT a quote, NOT an offer minimum)
facts.note: Supporting reference for the diversity/inclusion angle (pairs with QUOTE-007). NOT a quote. The '$250' in the URL slug is the article's framing, NOT a Birchal offer minimum: do NOT lift '$250' into ad copy as an investment minimum (BANNED-CSF-PHRASES + brief.md item 2 prohibit quoting any minimum pre-offer). Reference context only; cite no number from this.

facts.claim: Birchal Next: client-stated forward-looking positioning / vision for the modernised platform (mid 2026 onward)
facts.verbatim_text: From mid 2026, Birchal is doing things differently. We built the platform, now we're reinvigorating and modernising it: supreme analytics, excellent onboarding, fast time from prep to settlement, and a smooth, stress-free UX. It's next-generation. It's called Birchal Next. We're backed by X and Y, we have a new team, and we're turning a corner. We need help to do so.
facts.ad_safe_text: From mid 2026, Birchal is doing things differently. We built the platform, now we're modernising it. It's called Birchal Next.
facts.source: client email 2026-05-17, Kirstin Hunter, forward-looking positioning — NOT a verified product claim
facts.note: This is the client's stated VISION/positioning, framed forward-looking ('we're modernising', 'next-generation', 'turning a corner') — never as proven fact or current-state stat. Use future/aspirational voice only ('we're modernising', 'what's coming', 'the next chapter'), NEVER 'Birchal now has supreme analytics' or any present-tense capability claim. 'backed by X and Y' is a placeholder: must be replaced with the real, sourced named backers using ENDORSEMENT language only (rates/endorses/backs the model), never 'invested in us / backed this raise / on our cap table' — facts.json QUOTE-001..004 only support Pym/Carter/McKenzie endorsing Birchal + Gary backing Kirstin via Techstars, NOT investing in Birchal-the-company or this raise. All ad_safe rules apply: no en/em-dashes or smart quotes in shipped copy. 'next-generation' / 'supreme' are banned AI-tells exempt ONLY inside the attributed verbatim positioning, never in surrounding ad prose. Confidence MED because forward-looking, not a verified state.
